title: Lesson 34 – Following Instructions & Sequences
description: Anweisungen und Reihenfolgen: ～とおりに (gemäss, wie), V-た/V-dict + とおりに, ～あとで (nachdem), ～てから (nachdem man etw. getan hat).

vocabulary.meaning: to bake, to grill, to fry
to boil, to simmer, to stew

grammar.title: V た form / V dictionary form + とおりに (According to, as ~ did/does)
grammar.structure: V た + とおりに / V dict. + とおりに / N の + とおりに
grammar.explanation: ～とおりに means 'in the same way as ~', 'according to ~', or 'just as ~'. When used with the た form, it means 'do as someone did'. With the dictionary form, it means 'do as someone does/will do'. With a noun + の, it means 'according to the noun' (e.g., 説明書のとおりに — according to the manual). This pattern is essential for giving and following instructions.
grammar.example_sentences:
① 先生が 言ったとおりに してください。
  (Sensei ga itta toori ni shite kudasai.)
  — Please do as the teacher said.
② レシピの とおりに 作りました。
  (Reshipi no toori ni tsukurimashita.)
  — I made it according to the recipe.
③ 私が するとおりに してください。
  (Watashi ga suru toori ni shite kudasai.)
  — Please do it the way I do.

grammar.title: V た form + あとで (After doing ~)
grammar.structure: V た form + あとで / N の + あとで
grammar.explanation: ～たあとで means 'after doing ~'. It indicates that one action is completed before another begins. With nouns, use N のあとで. The key difference from ～てから is that ～たあとで simply states the time sequence, while ～てから emphasizes that the first action is a prerequisite for the second.
grammar.example_sentences:
④ ごはんを 食べたあとで、薬を 飲みます。
  (Gohan o tabeta ato de, kusuri o nomimasu.)
  — After eating, I take medicine.
⑤ 仕事のあとで、買い物に 行きます。
  (Shigoto no ato de, kaimono ni ikimasu.)
  — After work, I'll go shopping.

grammar.title: V て form + から (After doing ~, since doing ~)
grammar.explanation: ～てから means 'after doing ~' with emphasis on the sequence — the first action must be completed before moving to the second. It implies that the first action is a necessary step or prerequisite. Compared to ～たあとで, ～てから more strongly suggests a deliberate order of actions.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑥ 手を 洗ってから、料理を 始めましょう。
  (Te o aratte kara, ryouri o hajimemashou.)
  — Let's wash our hands and then start cooking.
⑦ 野菜を 切ってから、鍋に 入れてください。
  (Yasai o kitte kara, nabe ni irete kudasai.)
  — After cutting the vegetables, please put them in the pot.
⑧ 説明書を 読んでから、組み立ててください。
  (Setsumeisho o yonde kara, kumitatete kudasai.)
  — Please read the instructions before assembling it.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.japanese: 日本の カレーを 作りたいんですが、教えてもらえますか。
lines.romaji: Nihon no karee o tsukuritain desu ga, oshiete moraemasu ka.
lines.english: I'd like to make Japanese curry. Could you teach me?

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: いいですよ。まず、材料を 準備してから 始めましょう。
lines.romaji: Ii desu yo. Mazu, zairyou o junbi shite kara hajimemashou.
lines.english: Sure. First, let's prepare the ingredients and then start.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.japanese: はい。じゃがいもと にんじんは どう しますか。
lines.romaji: Hai. Jagaimo to ninjin wa dou shimasu ka.
lines.english: Okay. What do I do with the potatoes and carrots?

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: 皮を むいたあとで、小さく 切ってください。
lines.romaji: Kawa o muita ato de, chiisaku kitte kudasai.
lines.english: After peeling them, please cut them into small pieces.

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: 私が するとおりに してください。フライパンに 油を 入れて、肉を 焼きます。
lines.romaji: Watashi ga suru toori ni shite kudasai. Furaipan ni abura o irete, niku o yakimasu.
lines.english: Please do as I do. Put oil in the frying pan and cook the meat.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.japanese: 肉を 焼いてから、野菜も 入れますか。
lines.romaji: Niku o yaite kara, yasai mo iremasu ka.
lines.english: After cooking the meat, do I add the vegetables too?

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: はい、そのとおりです。全部 入れたあとで、水を 加えて 煮てください。
lines.romaji: Hai, sono toori desu. Zenbu ireta ato de, mizu o kuwaete nite kudasai.
lines.english: Yes, exactly. After putting everything in, add water and let it simmer.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.japanese: レシピのとおりに やってみます。ありがとうございます。
lines.romaji: Reshipi no toori ni yatte mimasu. Arigatou gozaimasu.
lines.english: I'll try doing it according to the recipe. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: 簡単ですから、大丈夫ですよ。
lines.romaji: Kantan desu kara, daijoubu desu yo.
lines.english: It's simple, so you'll be fine.
